mechanism_narrative: A sustained increase in the federal funds rate raises the discount rate applied to future corporate earnings, mechanically compressing equity valuations and producing negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains below 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window, or 20-day forward returns in tightening periods are not significantly lower than baseline (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: A sharp monthly increase in the unemployment rate signals deteriorating labor market conditions and rising recession risk, triggering risk-off positioning and negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 20-day forward returns in more than 60 percent of triggered instances despite the unemployment threshold breach.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous high inflation readings and rising policy rates compress real disposable income and equity multiples, producing negative S&P 500 forward returns over a 60-day horizon.
failure_condition: Forward returns show no significant difference when both series move in the specified direction simultaneously versus baseline over a 60-day window.

mechanism_narrative: Rising industrial production indicates expanding manufacturing capacity and robust aggregate supply, supporting corporate earnings and generating positive S&P 500 forward returns over a 5-day horizon.
failure_condition: 5-day forward returns decline when INDPRO rises for three consecutive months, contradicting the positive supply-demand channel.

mechanism_narrative: Increasing real savings balances reflect higher household liquidity, boosting consumption expectations and equity demand, which drives positive S&P 500 forward returns over 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Rank IC remains below 0.02 over any 2-year rolling window, or forward returns do not correlate positively with savings growth.

mechanism_narrative: A drop in housing starts below 1.1 million units signals weakening construction demand and broader economic slowdown, leading to negative S&P 500 forward returns over a 60-day horizon.
failure_condition: 60-day forward returns are not significantly lower when housing starts remain below the threshold for two consecutive months.

mechanism_narrative: Concurrent strong nonfarm payroll growth and stable unemployment rates confirm a healthy labor market expansion, reinforcing corporate revenue forecasts and driving positive S&P 500 forward returns over 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Forward returns turn negative when payroll growth exceeds 200k but unemployment rises simultaneously, breaking the labor market signal.

mechanism_narrative: When inflation exceeds 240 and policy rates are rising, real rate expectations spike, compressing equity valuations and producing negative S&P 500 forward returns over a 5-day horizon.
failure_condition: 5-day forward returns remain positive despite simultaneous inflation and rate increases, invalidating the real rate compression channel.
